 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-2vbk","title":"[IDEA-10] Event Threading Normalization Utilities","description":"# Event Threading Normalization Utilities

## Goal
Provide a normalized thread/topic representation across connectors (Telegram forums, Slack threads, Discord threads) to simplify downstream agent logic.

## Background / Why
Thread semantics differ across platforms; a unified schema reduces connector-specific branching and improves agent portability.

## Scope
- Add optional `thread` metadata to EventEnvelope and/or a helper struct.
- Provide helper to map platform-specific thread ids into normalized fields.

## Deliverables
- `ThreadInfo { thread_id, parent_id, kind }` helper + docs.
- Example mappings for Telegram forum topics and Discord/Slack threads.

## Tests
- Unit tests for mapping functions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Agents can rely on normalized thread metadata without platform-specific parsing.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add canonicalization proof tests: permute fields -> canonical bytes identical.
- Add deterministic vector generator with fixed seeds and algebraic edge cases.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Batch ed25519 verification and reuse hashers where safe.
- Avoid allocations in canonicalization using stack buffers.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","title":"[FCP3] Mesh first refoundation from host first truthful surface to a V3 native platform","description":"Background: the current repository is truthful but host first in day to day operation. The practical control path runs through crates/fwc/src/main.rs, crates/fwc/src/catalog.rs, crates/fwc/src/readiness.rs, and crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs, while crates/fcp-core/src/lib.rs still behaves like an overly broad semantic bucket and crates/fcp-protocol/src/lib.rs does not yet own the full FCPC and FCPS story.

Goal: make the platform genuinely mesh first in the sense intended by the V3 architecture, not merely more mesh flavored in naming or CLI copy. That means moving execution semantics, policy, evidence, durable truth, placement, leases, and failover into first class owners so that fcp-host becomes a node local root app over mesh native substrate layers and fwc can stay truthful while projecting mesh backed reality.

This initiative is the canonical migration graph. Success means: each major semantic noun has exactly one owner; FCPC is the normative live control and evidence plane; FCPS is the normative durable object plane; host local process state stops being the only source of truth for receipts, checkpoints, leases, and readiness; fwc consumes stable contracts instead of semantic buckets; and the cutover ends with deletion of transition scaffolding rather than permanent compatibility debt.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"initiative","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:00.136601Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-26T00:29:49.171173Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T00:29:49.170484Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","initiative","mesh-first"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-kipeo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:17.633269Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-nw3xn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:18.198810Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-q6fx6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:17.932261Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-rnphw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:17.323051Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-smptq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:18.895972Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-t1hy0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:19.195276Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-v2uw9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:18.612739Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2047,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","author":"CobaltHarbor","text":"This initiative exists to prevent a common failure mode: making the CLI or docs sound more mesh native before the runtime, durability, evidence, and placement layers actually are. If a future implementer has to choose between appearing more mesh first and staying strictly truthful about what the platform can prove, truthful provenance wins.

Use this graph as the self contained replacement for the spec derived migration narrative. The intended order is: freeze semantics, carve real owners, realign durable and wire truth, converge host and SDK, prove mesh placement and failover on a narrow pilot, move operator surfaces onto those contracts, then delete the transition scaffolding. No compatibility shims, no permanent host local truth islands, and no fake mesh claims.","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:00Z"},{"id":2055,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","author":"CobaltHarbor","text":"Graph hygiene note from creation pass on 2026-03-27: br dep cycles still reports 22 pre-existing cycles in older unrelated beads, but none of the new FCP3 initiative IDs created in this pass appear in that cycle set. This mesh-first initiative was added as an acyclic subgraph with one root initiative, seven phase epics, and thirty five task beads.","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:33:39Z"},{"id":2057,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","author":"CobaltHarbor","text":"Plan space revision on 2026-03-27: added the dedicated label fcp3-refoundation so bv analysis can isolate this initiative from older unrelated FCP3 beads. Also split the original P1.1 bottleneck into three parallel slice inventories plus one synthesis bead, and tightened the proof beads so unit tests, end to end scripts, structured logging, and artifact capture are explicit requirements rather than implied expectations.","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:22:44Z"},{"id":2066,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","author":"jemanuel","text":"Plan-space polish round on 2026-03-27: tightened Phase 7 so deletion can no longer outrun proof, added an explicit performance/resource proof bead for cutover-critical flows, made the transition scorecard guardrail-aware, and sharpened P4/P6 beads so connector-author UX, machine-readable CLI contracts, operator remediation, structured diagnostics, and reproducible logging are first-class requirements instead of implied nice-to-haves.","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:30:23Z"},{"id":2068,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","author":"jemanuel","text":"2026-03-28 polish round: added a dedicated pre-cutover baseline bead so performance and UX proof compare against preserved user-visible reality instead of memory after phase 7 deletions. Also tightened final review to wait on earlier phase closeout, and made docs/transcripts explicitly derive from acceptance and e2e artifacts so operator teaching stays honest.","created_at":"2026-03-28T05:14:13Z"},{"id":2071,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","author":"jemanuel","text":"2026-03-28 verification-hand-off polish: tightened the foundational semantic and runtime beads so they explicitly call for deterministic fixtures, transcripts, audit or trace fields, and compile-time or replayable evidence that later preservation, conformance, integration, and e2e beads will consume. This reduces the risk that verification remains implied instead of mechanically grounded.","created_at":"2026-03-28T05:38:32Z"},{"id":2889,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","author":"jemanuel","text":"All 7 phase epics closed: P1 rnphw (semantic lock), P2 kipeo (kernel/policy/evidence carve), P3 q6fx6 (FCPC/FCPS wire), P4 nw3xn (host+SDK convergence), P5 v2uw9 (placement/leases/failover), P6 smptq (CLI/operator truth), P7 t1hy0 (cutover + proof). FCP3 mesh-first refoundation complete with all proof artifacts published. Closing initiative.","created_at":"2026-04-26T00:29:48Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-2vvy","title":"[FCP2] InvokeRequest/InvokeResponse: Schema Validation, Holder Proof, Binding Rules","description":"# [FCP2] InvokeRequest/InvokeResponse: Schema Validation, Holder Proof, Binding Rules

## Goal
Implement the canonical InvokeRequest and InvokeResponse structures that are the primary interface for connector invocation.

## Why This Deserves Its Own Bead
The spec defines detailed requirements for InvokeRequest/InvokeResponse that span multiple concerns:
- capability token embedding and holder_proof generation
- idempotency_key handling
- lease binding (when required)
- evidence attachment rules
- response envelope structure

This complexity warrants focused implementation and testing.

## InvokeRequest (NORMATIVE)
Fields:
- `request_id` (UUID)
- `connector_id` (ConnectorId)
- `operation_id` (SchemaId)
- `zone_id` (ZoneId)
- `token` (CapabilityToken - COSE_Sign1 bytes)
- `holder_proof` (Ed25519 signature; REQUIRED when token.holder_node present)
- `idempotency_key` (Option<String>) - REQUIRED for Risky/Dangerous ops
- `inputs` (CBOR map - schema-validated)
- `context` (InvokeContext - trace_id, request_tags, etc.)
- `lease_seq` (Option<u64>) - REQUIRED for singleton_writer connectors

### holder_proof Binding (NORMATIVE)
When token has `holder_node` set:
- signable bytes MUST bind: `request_id || operation_id || token.jti`
- signature by holder node signing key
- prevents replay by non-holder nodes

### Idempotency Rules
- For `IdempotencyPolicy::Strict` or Risky/Dangerous: idempotency_key MUST be set
- Key format: opaque string, max 128 bytes

## InvokeResponse (NORMATIVE)
Fields:
- `request_id` (matches request)
- `status` (Ok | Error)
- `outputs` (Option<CBOR map> - schema-validated)
- `error` (Option<FcpError>)
- `receipt_id` (Option<ObjectId> - OperationReceipt)
- `audit_event_id` (Option<ObjectId>)
- `decision_receipt_id` (Option<ObjectId> - for denials)
- `response_metadata` (timing, cache hints, etc.)

## Subtasks
- Define InvokeRequest and InvokeResponse canonical schemas
- Implement holder_proof generation and verification
- Implement idempotency_key validation
- Implement lease_seq binding checks
- Define InvokeContext propagation rules

## Acceptance Criteria
- Golden vectors for InvokeRequest canonical encoding
- Adversarial tests: missing holder_proof rejected, invalid idempotency_key rejected
- Property tests: holder_proof binding covers all required fields

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","title":"fcp.airtable: Attachment upload and download handling","description":"# fcp.airtable: Attachment Upload and Download Handling

## Goal
Handle Airtable attachments safely under strict sandboxing.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Scope
Operations (representative):
- `airtable.attachments.upload`
- `airtable.attachments.get` (fetch attachment metadata/URL)
- optional: `airtable.attachments.download` (if we choose to implement actual download)

## Key requirements
- Capability gated:
  - read vs write separated
- NetworkConstraints:
  - Airtable API host(s) allowed
  - attachment download hosts must be explicitly enumerated/pinned
  - validate URLs are HTTPS and match allowlist
- Data handling:
  - attachments move as **ObjectId references** (no local disk persistence)
  - enforce size limits and streaming bounds
- Logging:
  - never log attachment contents
  - URLs may contain opaque tokens; treat as sensitive

## Tests
- Allow/deny matrix for attachment URL hosts.
- Size limit and timeout tests.
- Redaction tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Attachment flows are safe-by-default and deterministic.
- Non-allowed hosts are blocked by Network Guard.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"MossyShore","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:14:35.521843538Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:56.629767Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:56.629752Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-soft","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-soft.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1094,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","author":"MossyShore","text":"Implemented Airtable attachment download hardening. Changes: enforce HTTPS/host/port validation against the manifest allowlist, walk redirects manually with per-hop validation, cap response size at 100 MiB before/while reading, and stop logging token-bearing attachment URLs via tracing/error messages. Added focused tests for disallowed hosts, oversized downloads, local redirect handling, and FCP error mapping. Validation completed: rch exec -- cargo fmt --check; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/mossyshore-fcp-airtable-check cargo check -p fcp-airtable --all-targets; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/mossyshore-fcp-airtable-test cargo test -p fcp-airtable --test integration -- --nocapture; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/mossyshore-fcp-airtable-clippy cargo clippy -p fcp-airtable --all-targets -- -D warnings. Workspace-wide cargo check was attempted via rch but fell back locally and is still blocked/contended by concurrent cargo activity from other agents, so I am leaving the bead in_progress until that clears or someone explicitly accepts the package-scoped verification.","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:50:36Z"},{"id":1306,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented attachments.rs module: Attachment struct, AttachmentConfig (size/type limits), AttachmentValidation (URL/filename/MIME validation). 110 tests, all passing, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-g1s","title":"fcp.spotify: Search (Tracks, Albums, Artists, Podcasts)","description":"# fcp.spotify: Search (Tracks, Albums, Artists, Podcasts) (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide fast, typed Spotify search for discovery workflows.

## Scope
- Search across types:
  - track
  - album
  - artist
  - playlist
  - show/podcast
  - episode
- Support filters:
  - market/locale
  - pagination

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `spotify.search.read`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to Spotify Web API host.
- Treat titles/descriptions as tainted external content.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `spotify.search`
  - `spotify.search.tracks`
  - `spotify.search.albums`
  - `spotify.search.artists`
  - `spotify.search.shows`
  - `spotify.search.episodes`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - market handling
  - rate limiting + retry/backoff

## Acceptance criteria
- Search results are stable and correctly typed.

All with optional market parameter. 113 tests pass.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:40:33Z"},{"id":1358,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g1s","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented search.rs: SearchQuery builder, SearchPage<T> generic pagination, typed results for Track/Album/Artist/Playlist/Show/Episode, SearchValidator. 151 tests, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:45:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-g2c","title":"fcp.airtable: Full CRUD operations on records","description":"# fcp.airtable: Full CRUD Operations on Records

## Goal
Implement record CRUD with batching, pagination, and strict safety controls.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Scope
Operations (representative):
- `airtable.records.list` (pagination, filter/sort)
- `airtable.records.get`
- `airtable.records.create` / `airtable.records.create_batch`
- `airtable.records.update` / `airtable.records.update_batch`
- `airtable.records.upsert`
- `airtable.records.delete` / `airtable.records.delete_batch` (Dangerous)

## Key requirements
- Capability gated:
  - read vs write vs delete are separate capabilities
- Rate limiting and backpressure are mechanical.
- Pagination:
  - support continuation tokens
  - bound page sizes
- Batch operations:
  - handle partial failures deterministically
  - stable error mapping per record
- Idempotency / retries:
  - upsert is preferred for retry-safe workflows
  - deletes and schema writes must be treated as Dangerous (policy + receipts)

## Tests
- Mock-only tests cover:
  - pagination edge cases
  - batch partial failure semantics
  - upsert conflict handling
  - redaction: never log record bodies by default

## Acceptance Criteria
- CRUD operations behave deterministically under retries.
- Dangerous operations are properly gated and audited.
","notes":"In-progress implementation slice completed: added Airtable batch update, upsert, and batch delete surfaces across client/connector/manifest/tests. Package-level verification passed via rch for fmt/check/test/clippy.

Current state after this pass:
- new operations: airtable.update_records, airtable.upsert_records, airtable.delete_records
- new client helpers and response types added
- connector validation now enforces batch size and merge-field requirements for the new paths
- integration coverage added for client + connector invoke flows

Still pending for full bead closure:
- assess whether single-record CRUD should also resolve table refs through schema for stricter consistency
- consider deterministic partial-failure mapping / richer batch error surfaces if Airtable returns mixed mutation outcomes
- complete workspace-wide verification","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:14:33.751522695Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:56.494549Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T19:16:56.494536Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g2c","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-soft","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g2c","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-soft.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1054,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-g2c","author":"ChartreuseLake","text":"Implemented Airtable batch update/upsert/delete plus tightened single-record and batch-create connector paths to resolve table refs through cached schema and validate fields payload shape.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","title":"[FCP3/P3.2] Define canonical FCPS durable object schemas for runtime truth","description":"Background: a mesh first platform needs durable forms for the facts that survive process death and justify later decisions. Receipts, checkpoints, lease state, revocations, connector runtime state, and operator explanations cannot remain host local conventions.

Scope: define canonical FCPS object schemas for durable state, including connector identity and state, receipts, checkpoints, lease and fencing records, revocation records, and explanation bundles. Design them for replay, repair, inspection, and failover rather than as opaque storage details. Provide deterministic object examples, snapshot formats, explanation bundle shapes, schema unit tests, replay integration tests, and detailed inspection logs that later conformance and end to end harnesses can diff and diagnose.

Acceptance: the platform has a durable object vocabulary for the facts it must remember, those facts no longer depend on one host memory image to exist, and the schemas are concrete enough for fixture driven validation, replay, operator inspection, and regression tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:05.315492Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-03-28T06:31:09.065699Z","closed_at":"2026-03-28T06:31:09.065467Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","fcps","mesh-first","phase-3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-2m2hl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:24.219178Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-d8jbq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:24.654535Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2081,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","author":"jemanuel","text":"WhiteCompass completed 2026-03-28: Published FCPS durable object schemas at docs/FCP3_FCPS_Durable_Object_Schemas.md. Defines 7 object types: OperationReceipt (immutable execution proof), Checkpoint (state snapshot with lineage), Lease (exclusive ownership with fencing), Revocation (capability/session invalidation), ConnectorIdentity (deployment trust record), ExplanationBundle (human-readable decision justification), ConnectorState (durable runtime state). Common FcpsDurableObject header with schema versioning, content-addressing, and signatures. Includes fixtures, evolution rules, and inspection CLI commands.","created_at":"2026-03-28T06:31:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-gif","title":"fcp.pinecone: Pinecone Vector Database Connector","description":"# fcp.pinecone - Pinecone Vector Database Connector (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant connector for Pinecone (managed vector DB) supporting vector storage and semantic retrieval under strict zone isolation.

## Manifest archetypes (closed set)
- `storage`
- `knowledge`

## Zone model
Single-zone bound: one connector instance ↔ one `ZoneId`.

## External surface
### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only the configured Pinecone endpoint(s) and port(s) required by declared operations.

### Credential injection
- API key via `CredentialId`.
- Never persist or log keys.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `pinecone.index.manage`
- `pinecone.vectors.read`
- `pinecone.vectors.write`
- `pinecone.search.query`

## Operations (initial)
- index: create/describe/list/delete
- vectors: upsert/fetch/delete/query

## Tests
- Mock-only unit tests with wiremock.
- E2E compliance run validates default deny + NetworkConstraints + receipts for writes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner.
- Deterministic tests (no live Pinecone calls).

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-gif.1","title":"fcp.pinecone: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)","description":"# fcp.pinecone: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)

## Goal
Define the Pinecone connector manifest contract:
- archetypes (`storage`, `knowledge`)
- operations + schema IDs
- capability families + per-operation caps
- NetworkConstraints allowlist bound to configured endpoint

## Acceptance Criteria
- Complete manifest spec stored here.
- Good/bad manifest examples exist for static compliance.
